Title: Identification of oxidosqualene cyclases associated with saponin biosynthesis from Astragalus membranaceus reveals a conserved motif important for catalytic function

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Astragalus membranaceus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Astragalus membranaceus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to β-amyrin; also produces minor dammarenediol-II in Nicotiana benthamiana and epoxydammara-3,25-diol in yeast as minor by-products.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the scaffold precursor of oleanane-type saponins and soyasaponins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-defining scaffold-forming enzyme; yield determinant supported, but rate-limiting not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: β-amyrin; soyasaponin I; astroolesaponin D; robinioside B
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactive saponins; triterpenoid/saponin pharmacological activities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes β-amyrin accumulation and is associated with higher oleanane-type saponin/soyasaponin production in A. membranaceus roots.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AmOSC2 was cloned from A. membranaceus and biochemically confirmed as a β-amyrin synthase by transient expression in N. benthamiana, heterologous expression in yeast, and yeast microsome assays. 2. AmOSC2 is expressed much more strongly in underground tissues, matching higher β-amyrin and oleanane-type saponin accumulation. 3. Overexpression increased β-amyrin by 65.7% in A. membranaceus, whereas VIGS reduced it by 26.3%. 4. AmOSC2 transcript levels positively correlated with β-amyrin and oleanane-type saponins in roots. 5. Its nonconserved SIV triad lowers catalytic yield relative to the conserved VFM motif; SIV→VFM increased β-amyrin yield up to 12.8-fold.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana and A. membranaceus; heterologous expression in yeast GIL77; yeast microsome enzyme assay with 2,3-oxidosqualene; VIGS; RNA expression-metabolite correlation; site-directed mutagenesis; molecular docking
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher expression in underground parts/roots than aerial parts in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In A. membranaceus seedlings, strongly enriched in underground parts
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from NCBI SRA datasets SRR923811; ERR706814; SRR5343992; SRR5343993
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedlings grown at 25°C; 16 h/8 h light/dark or 12 h/12 h light/dark depending on assay; no special environmental factor central to metabolite interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression vector promoter system; GAL promoter in pYES2 yeast expression
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi of AmOSC2 triggered compensational increase of AmOSC3 expression; altered motif affects catalytic yield and by-product profile
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AmOSC2 carries an uncommon SIV motif instead of the conserved VFM found in most β-amyrin synthases. Single substitutions showed I728F had the strongest positive effect, and Y259A abolished activity. Minor products indicate some catalytic promiscuity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to engineer increased oleanane-type saponin production; replacing SIV with the conserved VFM motif can markedly enhance β-amyrin yield, supporting protein engineering for triterpenoid production.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Astragalus membranaceus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Astragalus membranaceus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cycloartane-type triterpenoid/astragaloside branch and sterol precursor branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the direct precursor for cycloartane-type astragalosides and also a precursor for plant sterols
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key scaffold-forming enzyme for cycloartane branch; not formally established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: cycloartenol; astragaloside I; astragaloside II; astragaloside III; astragaloside IV
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cycloartenol in seeds and seedlings broadly; astragalosides mainly in underground parts/roots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Astragalosides are major bioactive compounds in Huang-Qi with cardiovascular protective effects; cycloartenol also serves as a plant sterol precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cycloartenol accumulation and is associated with biosynthesis of cycloartane-type astragalosides in roots while also supporting sterol precursor formation in all tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AmOSC3 was cloned from A. membranaceus and biochemically confirmed as a cycloartenol synthase in N. benthamiana, yeast, and yeast microsome assays. 2. AmOSC3 is expressed in both aerial and underground tissues, with somewhat higher expression underground. 3. Overexpression increased cycloartenol by 27.1% in A. membranaceus, whereas VIGS reduced it by 61.0%. 4. AmOSC3 transcript levels positively correlated with cycloartenol and cycloartane-type saponins (astragalosides) in roots. 5. The conserved VFN motif is important for activity; mutation of VFN reduced AmOSC3 activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana and A. membranaceus; heterologous expression in yeast GIL77; yeast microsome enzyme assay with 2,3-oxidosqualene; VIGS; RNA expression-metabolite correlation; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all tested tissues, with higher expression in underground parts
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In A. membranaceus seedlings, present in both aerial and underground parts; cycloartenol distributed broadly, astragalosides enriched in roots
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from NCBI SRA datasets SRR923811; ERR706814; SRR5343992; SRR5343993
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedlings grown at 25°C; standard light/dark regimes; no special external treatment central to metabolite interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression vector promoter system; GAL promoter in pYES2 yeast expression
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing of AmOSC3 in hairy roots was reported as more likely detrimental, consistent with its essential contribution to sterol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AmOSC3 likely contributes both to specialized astragaloside biosynthesis and to core sterol metabolism. Downstream astragalosides accumulate specifically in underground tissues despite broader AmOSC3 expression.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering cycloartane-type triterpenoids/astragalosides, but manipulation may carry fitness penalties because cycloartenol is also a sterol precursor; catalytic activity can be tuned through the conserved VFN motif.
